item.title: Heavyweights Deliver a Spectacular Finale in Tashkent
item.description: The final day in Tashkent saw thrilling heavyweight action. Murad Fatiyev shocked Lasha Bekauri, Kaila Issoufi secured her first Grand Slam gold, Said Sadrudinov made history for Bahrain, and Akira Sone returned to the top. A dramatic finish closed an unforgettable weekend.

item.title: Four Nations Share Gold on Day Two in Tashkent
item.description: Day two of the OTP Group Tashkent Grand Slam 2026 saw gold medals claimed by four different nations. Tanioka dominated at -63kg, Abdulloi stunned the home crowd at -73kg, Butkereit impressed in -70kg, and Rajabli secured his first Grand Slam gold at -81kg in front of a passionate Uzbek audience.

item.title: Home Gold Shines on Day 1 in Tashkent
item.description: Day one of the OTP Group Tashkent Grand Slam 2026 delivered thrilling action. Hui Xinran claimed -48kg gold, Abe Hifumi stunned with the throw of the day, and Shukurjon Aminova secured Uzbekistan’s first gold. A bronze from Doston Ruziev capped a proud start for the host nation.

item.title: Napoli fan 'stabbed by wife' at home after VAR decision sparks domestic dispute
item.description: The 40-year-old man was stabbed by his wife after a misunderstanding while watching a Napoli game at their home, according to Italian media.

item.title: UEFA suspends Benfica's Prestianni for one match over alleged racist abuse towards Vinícius Júnior
item.description: UEFA has provisionally banned Benfica forward Gianluca Prestianni after allegations he racially abused Real Madrid’s Vinícius Júnior in last week’s Champions League clash. Benfica heads into the second leg on Wednesday trailing a goal and without manager José Mourinho, who is serving a suspension.

item.title: Milano-Cortina Games end: How Europe saw Italy’s Winter Olympics
item.description: As the Milano-Cortina Winter Olympic Games wrapped up, many European countries returned home with medals. Euronews bureaus across Europe report on how the Games were received in their respective countries.

item.title: EU tops combined medal standings at Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics
item.description: When tallied, the results of all EU member states placed the 27-member European bloc at the top of the overall medal standings at the Milano-Cortina 2026 Winter Olympics.

item.title: Norway tops the medals count as Milan-Cortina Olympics close in Verona Arena
item.description: The next Winter Games will be held in France, which received the Olympic flag in the official handover in the closing ceremony.

item.title: Alcaraz stays perfect in 2026 with dominant Doha title run
item.description: World No. 1 Carlos Alcaraz improved to 12–0 in 2026, defeating Arthur Fils 6–2, 6–1 to win the Qatar Open, while Fils capped a breakthrough week with a runner-up finish in Doha.

item.title: Italian broadcaster's sports chief quits after series of Olympics on-air gaffes
item.description: RaiSport chief Paolo Petrecca quit after a series of blunders during the Milano-Cortina Olympics opening ceremony sparked revolt and possible strikes by RAI journalists.

item.title: Racism is not rivalry. It is a crime
item.description: There are lines that cannot be blurred. Racism is one of them. No context can mitigate it, no club passion can excuse it, no victory can relativise it. We cannot tolerate intolerance, Bruno Batista writes in an opinion article for Euronews.

item.title: UEFA set to probe racism row after Real Madrid's Vinícius Jr claims abuse in Benfica match
item.description: Vinícius accuses Benfica’s Prestianni of racist abuse in Real Madrid’s win in Lisbon on Tuesday night, halting play for 10 minutes as UEFA considers an investigation into the incident.

item.title: Cheating row at Winter Olympics challenges curling's culture of trust
item.description: A cheating row over "double-touching" has rattled curling at the Winter Olympics, testing the sport's culture of trust and self-officiating.

item.title: Alcaraz and Sinner set for Doha showdown as Qatar ExxonMobil Open returns
item.description: Carlos Alcaraz and Jannik Sinner headline the Qatar ExxonMobil Open in Doha, recently voted ATP 500 Tournament of the Year. With Novak Djokovic withdrawing, the spotlight shifts firmly to the sport's new generation.

item.title: Lucas Pinheiro Braathen makes history for Brazil after winning giant slalom gold in Milano Cortina
item.description: Lucas Pinheiro Braathen beat Switzerland's Marco Odermatt by 0.58 seconds in the giant slalom event to clinch the gold and with it, securing Brazil's first ever medal in the Winter Olympics.

item.title: The harder the fall... The most impressive images of the falls at the 2026 Olympic Games
item.description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most spectacular falls of athletes at the Milan Winter Olympics.

item.title: Mikhail Shaidorov wins historic figure skating Olympic gold for Kazakhstan at Milano Cortina games
item.description: Mikhail Shaidorov became Kazakhstan’s first Olympic gold medallist in men’s figure skating after triumphing at the Milano Cortina Winter Olympics 2026.

item.title: Eat like an Olympian: what athletes need to eat during the Games and how nutrition helps performance
item.description: During the Winter Olympic Games, thousands of athletes have breakfast, lunch, and dinner in the facilities. What are they eating, and what role does nutrition play in the competition?

item.title: 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics: Who leads the medal count?
item.description: Which country is doing best at this year's Winter Olympics, held in Italy's Milan and Cortina d'Ampezzo? Track the official medal standings as they are updated throughout the Games.

item.title: At Milano-Cortina Olympics, curling turns into true family affair on ice
item.description: Married couples and siblings turn emotion into an edge in Milano-Cortina’s mixed doubles curling, balancing heated on-ice clashes with family life and rituals.

item.title: Thank You, Paris! Day 2 Highlights from the Grand Slam 2026
item.description: The final day in Paris delivered top-level judo and unforgettable moments. Gold medals for Oino, Ozbas, Tajima, Bellandi, Arai, Dicko and Nakano thrilled the crowd, as Paris once again proved why it is the heart of world judo.

item.title: Electric Start: Day 1 Highlights from the Paris Grand Slam 2026
item.description: Day 1 of the Paris Grand Slam delivered top-level judo and unforgettable moments. Home hero Shirine Boukli thrilled the crowd, while gold medals also went to Aghayev, Krasniqi, Takeoka, Cysique, Makhmadbekov and Silva in a spectacular opening day at Accor Arena.

item.title: Milan protesters rally against environmentally and economically 'unsustainable' Winter Olympics
item.description: A demonstration took place in Milan on the first day of the 2026 Milan-Cortina Winter Olympics, with protesters lamenting the environmental and economic effect of the Games on the region.

item.title: Winter Olympics officially open in Milan and Cortina, with two cauldrons lit to start the games
item.description: The ceremony’s organisers have said they sought to represent the city-mountain dichotomy of the particularly unusual setup for these Olympics while also trying to appeal to a sense of unity at a time of global tensions.

item.title: Size matters: Alleged hyaluronic acid penis injections may be helping Olympic ski jumpers
item.description: The World Anti-Doping Agency will look into allegations that ski jumpers are injecting hyaluronic acid into their penises to gain aerodynamic advantage at the Winter Olympics 2026.

item.title: When the Saints go marching in: NFL to stage first ever American Football game in France
item.description: France is about to experience its first ever professional NFL American Football game with the New Orleans Saints scheduled to play the 2026/27 season opener in Paris.

item.title: Alcaraz overcomes injury to defeat Zverev in thrilling five-set Australian Open semi-final
item.description: The five-set match set the record for the longest this year and the third in Australian Open history.

item.title: UN calls for 52-day pause in global wars ahead of Milan Cortina Winter Olympics
item.description: UN truce resolutions typically pass with broad majorities and received all 193 votes for the London Games in 2012. Yet signatories have repeatedly broken their own promises.

item.title: Uzbekistan hosts major international sports meetings, strengthens regional role
item.description: More than 360 senior sports officials visited Tashkent as Asia’s Olympic leadership changed hands, new cooperation agreements were signed, and international federations inspected cutting-edge facilities, signalling rising confidence in Uzbekistan’s sporting infrastructure and organisation.

item.title: US rock climber Alex Honnold reaches top of Taipei 101 skyscraper without ropes
item.description: Alex Honnold ascended the Taipei 101 skyscraper without ropes, using small outcroppings as footholds. The climb, broadcast live on Netflix, took around 90 minutes.

item.title: Fantasy futsal: Azerbaijan look to impress indoors as popularity of skillful sport soars
item.description: Futsal, the fast-paced, small-sided version of indoor football, has grown into a truly global sport, played in over 100 countries and enjoyed by more than 30 million players worldwide, according to FIFA.

item.title: Qatar Football Festival 2026 brings Messi, Yamal, Salah and top teams to Doha in March
item.description: Qatar Football Festival 2026 will be headlined by the men’s Finalissima between Argentina and Spain, with some of the world’s biggest football stars set to appear in Doha during a six-match international window.

item.title: Senegal stun Morocco in AFCON 2025 final to retain continental crown
item.description: AFCON 2025 concluded with a dramatic finale as Senegal defeated hosts Morocco to be crowned champions of Africa, capping a tournament filled with surprises, emotion and unforgettable moments on and off the pitch.

item.title: Nigeria take bronze as Morocco and Senegal set up AFCON final
item.description: As AFCON 2025 heads towards a thrilling finale in Morocco, the host cities are buzzing with far more than football. In Casablanca and Rabat, art, culture and the passion of fans are turning the tournament into a continent‑wide celebration.

item.title: Atlas Lions roar into AFCON final to set up showpiece clash with Senegal
item.description: Morocco is in full celebration mode as the Africa Cup of Nations reaches its decisive stage, with football fever gripping both Casablanca and Rabat.

item.title: Nigeria, Egypt complete AFCON semi-final lineup
item.description: Mohamed Salah scored and Egypt eliminated defending champion Ivory Coast to move into the Africa Cup of Nations semifinals after a 3-2 victory on Saturday.

item.title: Morocco, Senegal roar to AFCON semis
item.description: Iliman Ndiaye’s first-half strike was enough for Senegal to beat 10-man Mali 1-0 in the first quarterfinal. Brahim Díaz scored again and host Morocco joined Senegal in the semifinals of the Africa Cup of Nations with a 2-0 win over Cameroon on Friday.

item.title: AFCON 2025 : Algeria, Côte d’Ivoire cap quarterfinal lineup
item.description: The Africa Cup of Nations quarterfinal lineup was finalized Tuesday with Algeria and defending champion Ivory Coast booking the last places.

item.title: Salah shines for Egypt and Nigeria beat Mozambique to advance
item.description: On the last day of the Round of 16, Algeria meet DR Congo and Côte d’Ivoire battle neighbors Burkina Faso.

item.title: Manchester United sacks manager Ruben Amorim after 14 months at Old Trafford
item.description: The Portuguese oversaw a slew of unwanted records at the 20-time English champion including its lowest finish in the Premier League era last season.

item.title: Cameroon edge South Africa, to face Morocco next
item.description: Monday's fixtures [Round of 16] : Egypt vs. Benin and Nigeria vs. Mozambique.

item.title: Senegal, Mali set up AFCON quarterfinal clash
item.description: Sadio Mané led Senegal to the quarterfinals of the Africa Cup of Nations after a 3-1 win over Sudan in Tangier on Saturday.

item.title: 'Why am I doing this?': Stefanos Tsitsipas reveals constant pain almost forced him to quit tennis
item.description: The Greek tennis player and former world number three opened his heart at a press conference ahead of the start of the United Cup in Perth, Australia

item.title: British boxer Anthony Joshua staying in Nigeria to recuperate after car crash, promoter says
item.description: A vehicle carrying the former heavyweight champion and two of his associates crashed into a stationary truck on Monday on a major thoroughfare, the Lagos-Ibadan Expressway, which links Ogun state to Lagos.

item.title: Morocco welcomes 2026 as AFCON enters knockout stage
item.description: Morocco welcomed the New Year with celebrations across the country. Streets and public squares came alive with music, lights, and crowds gathering as 2025 drew to a close.

item.title: Streaming platform DAZN to continue Belgian league broadcasts after arbitration ruling
item.description: DAZN announced last month it had no choice but to let its contract expire under Belgian law because it was impossible to fulfil the distribution condition of the deal.

item.title: World's oldest professional football player signs for one more season
item.description: Japan's Kazuyoshi "King Kazu" Miura, who turns 59 in February, joined third-division Fukushima United on loan from Yokohama FC.

item.title: AFCON 2025: Nigeria perfect as knockout picture sharpens
item.description: The Africa Cup of Nations group stage moved closer to its conclusion as Groups C and D wrapped up decisive final matches, with qualification places and knockout pairings taking shape across Morocco.

item.title: From Usyk to Infantino, top summit draws hundreds of sports figures to Dubai
item.description: The inaugural World Sports Summit in the UAE featured Usyk, Pacquiao, Infantino, and other top stars and decision-makers discussing athlete pressures alongside the sport's economic and social future.

item.title: AFCON 2025: Morocco cruise through as knockout picture takes shape
item.description: Matchday three of the Africa Cup of Nations group stage delivered clarity and celebration in Morocco, as decisive results in Groups A and B set the stage for the knockout phase.
